lines.text: A place this intricate is difficult to fully comprehend in such a short time. You seem to know this place like the back of your hand. Have you done some research prior to your last visit?
I see. One moment, I'll add a few final notes here.

The Fallen Grave relies heavily on frequency decomposers. Only a small number of Echoes maintained their operation, and no personnel appeared during the course of the investigation. Future management may require fewer staff.
Evaluating and handling anomalies in the Common Echoes requires further revision. They should be offered a choice, rather than helplessly be sent on a conveyor belt for disassembly and smelting.
Are we now inside the Fabricatorium? This area is...
Coldness and death litter these obscure corners.
Only a few straggler Echoes remain, watching their own kind being fed into the "furnace" one by one.
We've always regarded Common Echoes as gifts from the Sentinel, never caring about their birth or death. But here lies the dissolving and decaying truth behind the miracles.
You're saying frequency energy can be reused? You mean there's a way for these abnormal Echoes to be reborn after meeting their end?
Where does that energy lead? Do the reborn Echoes retain their past consciousness?
You speak in riddles. I suppose I'll have to take notes more carefully.
...It seems you haven't reached the deeper truth. This place is more than just a factory for Echoes.
The new research data I gathered indicates the Order has been secretly pushing experiments on Echo consciousness control in the facility.
In other words, if things had gone according to the Order's plans, the Echoes would become mere puppets and watchdogs entirely under their control.
But... we should've led with faith, not attempting control through force.
If all we have is sand slipping through our fingers, no matter how tightly we grasp, nothing would have mattered. Why, Primus Fenrico, why must you do this...
Ah, apologies, I tend to talk to myself sometimes... Let's move on and document this place.
The amount of energy stored here is astounding.
The data shows two types of energy being stored here.
One of them matches what we found in the Fallen Grave. Recycled Echo frequencies must be stored here before being distributed to the incubation areas.
The other energy source appeared on devices we saw before. It seems to be the power source that supports the Fabricatorium's operations.
But why is it giving a low energy level reading...
Explosion?
So there's an underground section of the facility. And that's where the energy storage area is located?
Let's go take a look.
These Echoes have less stable consciousness than ordinary Common Echoes. The experiment here is truly a chore.
It's not wise to hold everything too tightly. Sometimes, letting go brings greater rewards. An experiment that does only harm may not be worth continuing...
As it stands, Ragunna still needs a place where the Echoes can call home...
I've noticed and recorded an image of it.
Do you remember the melody?
What a beautiful song. As if I can almost hear a story from it.
I have it recorded. Seeing how focused you are, I took the liberty of capturing your image.
Your guidance has been essential to my understanding of this place. Trust me, we will make this place better.
The age of lies is behind us now. Now that the shadow over the Order has lifted, I'm sure the brighter days will come.
Hmm? Why are you looking at me like that?
Heh, isn't that good? Ambition, or you might call it, a desire for the better.
The Order of the Deep needs change. Ragunna deserves a better future, but who will lead that change, I'm not sure.
Looks like the path is sealed. We'll need someone to repair the underground levels first.
Hmm? What are these?
They are not entirely about Echoes. And the data seems to be from another era.
No, wait, given the age of these records, the conclusions about the Waveworn Phenomenon were far ahead of their time.
Mind if we look over this again? I want to make sure we don't miss any critical information.
Alessio, why bother? Look at yourself. You're a mess.
lines.quest_name: Morning Star: Those Who Remain in the Night Part II

lines.text: The Order stands without a Primus or Priest, and it will remain so for the foreseeable future, Alessio.
lines.quest_name: Morning Star: Those Who Remain in the Night Part II

lines.text: Acolytes Barlin and Gnessa have both sacrificed themselves. Martyred, along with the Sentinel.
lines.quest_name: Morning Star: Those Who Remain in the Night Part II

lines.text: Look around. Two prison escorts flank us. One for you, one for me. It's time we stand trial for our sins.
lines.quest_name: Morning Star: Those Who Remain in the Night Part II

lines.text: Each and every citizen of Ragunna is qualified to judge you and me, Alessio.
lines.quest_name: Morning Star: Those Who Remain in the Night Part II

lines.text: If the Primus indeed fell to the Threnodian's corruption, and lower-ranking Acolytes are proven to have been deceived, then as the Order's voice and ears, we must face the reckoning.
lines.quest_name: Morning Star: Those Who Remain in the Night Part II

lines.text: Get up, Alessio. Ragunna is waiting.
lines.quest_name: Morning Star: Those Who Remain in the Night Part II

lines.text: ...You disgust me, Alessio. Since when did you start believing that Ragunna was ours to own?
lines.quest_name: Morning Star: Those Who Remain in the Night Part II

lines.text: Let's go. I've already made the arrangements. The people will count your crimes, then mine.
lines.quest_name: Morning Star: Those Who Remain in the Night Part II
